dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need your help. I've done something terrible, and I did it out of love.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My child wandered into the woods {location_hint} last month and came back with nightmares about the {enemy_type}. I was so afraid for their safety that I barricaded the eastern road and diverted the night patrols to watch our home. What I didn't realize is that I left the southern approach to {location} completely undefended. Raiders slipped through three nights ago and stole the settlement's entire stock of {quest_item}. {target_npc} is furious, and rightfully so. Those supplies were meant for everyone.
dialogueChunks.outro: I was trying to protect my family. Instead, I endangered everyone else's. I need to make this right.

playerResponses.text: Parents make mistakes. I'll recover the supplies and we'll sort the rest out later.
Good intentions, bad consequences. I'll help, but the settlement deserves an explanation.
You weakened your own settlement's defenses. That's on you.

dialogueChunks.intro: Please, {player_name}, hear me out before you judge. I know how this looks.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} is my dearest friend, and when they fell ill last week, I panicked. I took {quest_item} from the communal medicine stores without asking: enough to brew a private remedy. It worked: {target_npc} recovered in two days. But now three other people are sick with the same ailment and there isn't enough medicine left for any of them. The alchemist says the only way to replace what I took is to gather fresh ingredients from the marshes {location_hint}, but the {enemy_type} have been breeding there.
dialogueChunks.outro: I saved my friend and condemned three strangers. I can't live with that. Please help me fix it.

playerResponses.text: The sick need medicine. I'll get what the alchemist requires.
You stole communal supplies for one person. The marsh trip is the least you owe.
Medicine shortages aren't my specialty. Talk to the alchemist.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need to confess something. And then I need your help to make it right.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I've been secretly feeding {target_npc}'s family from the tavern stores for weeks. They lost everything in the last storm and were too proud to ask for help. I thought I was being kind. But now the tavern stores are depleted and the harvest festival is in three days. The whole settlement depends on that feast for morale through the dark months. Without {quest_item} to restock, there's no festival and everyone will know someone has been skimming. The nearest source is {location_hint}, but that stretch of road has been plagued by {enemy_type}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I was trying to help one family and ended up betraying the trust of an entire settlement. Please, help me set this right before anyone finds out.

playerResponses.text: Generosity that went too far. I'll restock the stores before anyone notices.
You should have asked the community for help openly. But fine, I'll get the supplies.
Your secret charity, your problem. Come clean and deal with it.

dialogueChunks.intro: I thought I was protecting them, {player_name}. That's all I ever wanted to do.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When I heard rumors about {enemy_type} scouts near {location}, I convinced {target_npc} to move their family to a farmstead outside the walls where I thought they'd be safer. I even helped them carry their belongings. Last night, a raiding party hit the farmstead. {target_npc}'s family escaped, but everything they owned: tools, {quest_item}, winter stores: is gone. They had to flee {location_hint}, and now the raiders are using the farmstead as a forward camp. I moved them straight into danger.
dialogueChunks.outro: Their possessions, their security: I took it all away with the best of intentions. If someone doesn't recover what was lost and clear that farmstead, the damage I've done is permanent.

playerResponses.text: An occupied farmstead and stolen supplies? I'll clear the camp and bring back what I can.
You meant well but made things worse. Story of this settlement. I'll fix it.
You moved them outside the walls? That was reckless. Deal with the fallout yourself.
